Ending Chronic Homelessness in Waterloo Region Launch Event
You are invited to the extra special National Housing Day celebration this
year. At the event, ALL IN 2020: Ending Chronic Homelessness in
Waterloo Region will be launched as a joint initiative of Wellbeing
Waterloo Region and Homelessness and Housing Umbrella
The event is taking place as follows:
Location: The Centre for International Governance Innovation
If you are planning to attend, please RSVP to Ray Hoover at 519-575-4400 X 4679 or
As part of ALL IN 2020: Ending Chronic Homelessness in Waterloo Region, a series of
ads will be in the Record over the months of November and December.
A huge thank you to Kitchener Waterloo Community Foundation for donating the ad
space. The ALL IN 2020 section of the ads will feature 6 different community leader
and will profile each of housing heros. The first ad will be published on November 3rd
Affordable Housing Catalyst Group & Action Groups
Affordable Housing Catalyst group had its first meeting in July. Over twenty five The
people from various sectors and backgrounds who are passionate about ending chronic
homelessness in Waterloo Region are participating on this Catalyst Group. To date, the
group has met four times and has already accomplished a lot, highlights include:
• Helping to plan for the launch of the WWR and Homelessness and Housing
Umbrella Group (HHUG) joint initiative: ALL IN 2020: Ending Chronic
• Providing input on the series of newspaper ads that will be published in the
• Considering a presentation on how to create a fundraising plan and beginning to
Three small action groups have also formed to specifically focus on a communication
strategy and the development of a data dashboard.
How do we measure quality of life in our Communities?
Having standardized, reliable data is key to understanding how well a
community is doing. There are measurement systems that can help our
community do this work. But how do we mix and match the most appropriate
Come out to a workshop to:
• Learn more about the measurement systems being used in Waterloo Region.
• Learn about the Canadian Index of Wellbeing, the Sustainable Development
Goals and the World Council on City Data.

To find out more and to print the poster invitation click here.
We have created a community calendar so you can view upcoming events that are
connected to wellbeing across Waterloo Region. We encourage community partners to
send upcoming events to WWR backbone staff so we can make this calendar as up to
date as possible. It can also be a place to look when you are planning large cross
sector events or meetings in order to avoid time conflicts.
We have a working group that has been working hard to follow up on the expression of
interest process and will bring the list of potential members for each of the working
groups to the Interim Reference group at the end of November. To find out more about
of each of the groups click here.
